mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 4% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high UNRATE regimes exceed 1.5% monthly volatility or fail to show significant negative correlation (Pearson < -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal stagflation, causing equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in stagflation scenarios show positive correlation (Pearson > 0.15) with combined GDPC1/CPIAUCSL signals.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 5%, compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high FEDFUNDS regimes exceed 1.2% monthly volatility or show positive correlation (Pearson > 0.1) with rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS environments show negative correlation (Pearson < -0.15) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to S&P 500 underperformance over 60 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high CPIAUCSL regimes exceed 1.8% monthly volatility or show positive correlation (Pearson > 0.1) with inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding supply-side capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in combined INDPRO/HOUST growth scenarios show negative correlation (Pearson < -0.1) or Rank IC < 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Supply chain disruptions, indicated by declining industrial production (INDPRO) below 100, reduce corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in INDPRO contraction periods exceed 1.5% monthly volatility or show positive correlation (Pearson > 0.1) with production declines.
